name: docdoc
description: Automatically generate and update Python docstrings in Google, NumPy, or Sphinx format.

commands.title: Python: Generate All Docstrings for File
Python: Update All Docstrings in File
Python: Generate and Update All Docstrings in File
Python: Convert All Docstrings in File

enumDescriptions: Auto-detect from pyproject.toml; default to Google style if no docstring setting found

docdoc.onSave.enable.description: Generate docstrings for all undocumented functions and classes on file save.

docdoc.includeTypesFromAnnotations.description: Include data type info in the docstring when a type annotation is present in the function or class signature.

docdoc.includeDefaults.description: Append 'Defaults to X.' in the parameter description when a default parameter value is present.

enumDescriptions: Always include a Returns or Yields section.
Only include a Returns section when the function body returns a value or has a non-None return annotation.
docdoc.returns.mode.description: When to include a Returns (or Yields) section in the docstring.

docdoc.generateModuleDocstring.description: When running the  'Generate All Docstrings for File' command, also insert a module-level docstring if the file does not already have one.

docdoc.placeholders.summary.description: Placeholder text for the docstring's summary line.

docdoc.placeholders.description.description: Placeholder text for the docstring's parameter and return descriptions.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-generateDocstring
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate a Python docstring for a function or class at the specified line. Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

line.description: The 0-based line number of the function or class. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

format.description: The docstring format to use. Defaults to the extension configuration.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-generateAllDocstrings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate docstrings for all undocumented functions and classes in one or more Python files. Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

format.description: The docstring format to use. Defaults to the extension configuration.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-updateDocstring
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Update an existing docstring to match the current function or class signature. Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

line.description: The 0-based line number of the function or class. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-updateAllDocstrings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Update all docstrings in one or more Python files to match their current signatures. Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-generateAndUpdateAllDocstrings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate missing docstrings and update existing ones in one or more Python files. Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

format.description: The docstring format to use. Defaults to the extension configuration.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-convertDocstring
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Convert an existing docstring to a different format (Google, NumPy, or Sphinx). Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

line.description: The 0-based line number of the function or class. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

languageModelTools.name: docdoc-convertAllDocstrings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Convert all docstrings in one or more Python files to a different format (Google, NumPy, or Sphinx). Supports batch processing via the `files` array. Files are modified directly.

uri.description: The file URI or path of a single Python file. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.

files.description: An array of file URIs or paths to process in batch. Use either `uri` or `files`, not both.
